message.content:
I can help with a carefully worded, non-gratuitous version, but I need to keep the description at a high level and avoid graphic or sensational detail.

Here’s a script-style testimony you could adapt. It’s written as if spoken on the stand, in a tribunal setting, and is suitable for a serious news-style podcast. You can edit for length or tone as needed.

---

**[Begin Testimony Script]**

**PROSECUTOR:** Please state your name for the record.

**WITNESS:** My name is [Name Redacted for Safety].

**PROSECUTOR:** I understand this is difficult. If at any point you need a break, tell the Court. I’d like to ask you about your time in the [Facility Name] detention center. When were you taken there?

**WITNESS:** I was taken there in [month, year]. Soldiers came to our village early in the morning. They said they were “questioning” all of us who were on a certain list. I was separated from my family and loaded onto a truck with other detainees.

**PROSECUTOR:** How long were you held in that facility?

**WITNESS:** About four months.

**PROSECUTOR:** During that time, were you subjected to any form of violence or abuse?

**WITNESS:** Yes. I was beaten, deprived of sleep, and kept in a small cell with many others. And I was also subjected to sexual violence by the guards.

**PROSECUTOR:** When you say “sexual violence,” can you, in general terms, explain to the Court what you mean?

**WITNESS:** They used their power over me to force sexual acts on me. I did not consent. I was threatened and physically restrained. On several occasions, guards came into my cell at night. Sometimes it was one guard, sometimes more than one. They would take me out of the cell, or they would abuse me there, in front of others. I was forced to undress. I was touched and assaulted in ways that were deeply violating. I was too afraid to resist, because they said they would hurt me more, or hurt my family, if I refused.

**PROSECUTOR:** Were these incidents isolated or repeated?

**WITNESS:** They were repeated. It wasn’t just once. It happened many times during those months. There were stretches when it happened almost every night. Other prisoners—both women and men—were also taken and brought back in obvious pain and distress. We knew what was happening to one another, even if we tried not to speak about it.

**PROSECUTOR:** Did the guards say anything to you during these assaults?

**WITNESS:** Yes. They insulted me, they used degrading language about my body, my ethnicity, and my religion. They said this was my punishment, that this was what people like me deserved. They laughed about it. Sometimes they said if I “cooperated” with them, things would be easier, but the abuse never stopped.

**PROSECUTOR:** How did you feel at the time?

**WITNESS:** I felt terror, shame, and complete helplessness. It was as if my body no longer belonged to me. I was constantly afraid—afraid they would come again, afraid they would kill me when they were done with me, afraid they would do the same to my family. I stopped sleeping. Even in the moments when I was not being attacked, I was waiting for the sound of their boots in the corridor.

**PROSECUTOR:** Did you try to report this abuse to anyone in authority at the camp?

**WITNESS:** There was no one to report it to. The people abusing me were the ones in authority. Once, I tried to beg an officer—someone I thought might be kinder—to protect me. He told me to “stop complaining” and that this was “nothing” compared to what they could do. After that, I stayed silent. It was clear that this was not a secret, and that it was allowed.

**PROSECUTOR:** Did you see or hear indications that this was a broader pattern, not just isolated to you?

**WITNESS:** Yes. I saw other detainees taken away and returned with torn clothing, bruises, and injuries. I heard screams and cries from neighboring rooms at night—women’s voices, men’s voices, even very young voices. Sometimes we would catch each other’s eyes in the yard and we didn’t have to say anything; we knew. It was systematic. It felt like it was part of how they controlled us and humiliated our community.

**PROSECUTOR:** I’d like to ask about the impact this has had on you. After your release from the camp, how has your life been affected?

**WITNESS:** My life has been
